kjv.PRO_25_19: Confidence in an unfaithful man in time of trouble is like a broken tooth, and a foot out of joint.
kjv.PRO_25_20: As he that taketh away a garment in cold weather, and as vinegar upon nitre, so is he that singeth songs to an heavy heart.
kjv.PRO_25_21: If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he be thirsty, give him water to drink:
kjv.PRO_25_22: For thou shalt heap coals of fire upon his head, and the Lord shall reward thee.
kjv.PRO_25_23: The north wind driveth away rain: so doth an angry countenance a backbiting tongue.
kjv.PRO_25_24: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman and in a wide house.
kjv.PRO_25_25: As cold waters to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.
kjv.PRO_25_26: A righteous man falling down before the wicked is as a troubled fountain, and a corrupt spring.
kjv.PRO_25_27: It is not good to eat much honey: so for men to search their own glory is not glory.
kjv.PRO_25_28: He that hath no rule over his own spirit is like a city that is broken down, and without walls.
kjv.PRO_26_1: As snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honour is not seemly for a fool.
kjv.PRO_26_2: As the bird by wandering, as the swallow by flying, so the curse causeless shall not come.
kjv.PRO_26_3: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the fool's back.
kjv.PRO_26_4: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him.
kjv.PRO_26_5: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own conceit.
kjv.PRO_26_6: He that sendeth a message by the hand of a fool cutteth off the feet, and drinketh damage.
kjv.PRO_26_7: The legs of the lame are not equal: so is a parable in the mouth of fools.
kjv.PRO_26_8: As he that bindeth a stone in a sling, so is he that giveth honour to a fool.
kjv.PRO_26_9: As a thorn goeth up into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouths of fools.
kjv.PRO_26_10: The great God that formed all things both rewardeth the fool, and rewardeth transgressors.
kjv.PRO_26_11: As a dog returneth to his vomit, so a fool returneth to his folly.
kjv.PRO_26_12: Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
kjv.PRO_26_13: The slothful man saith, There is a lion in the way; a lion is in the streets.
kjv.PRO_26_14: As the door turneth upon his hinges, so doth the slothful upon his bed.
kjv.PRO_26_15: The slothful hideth his hand in his bosom; it grieveth him to bring it again to his mouth.
kjv.PRO_26_16: The sluggard is wiser in his own conceit than seven men that can render a reason.
kjv.PRO_26_17: He that passeth by, and meddleth with strife belonging not to him, is like one that taketh a dog by the ears.
kjv.PRO_26_18: As a mad man who casteth firebrands, arrows, and death,
kjv.PRO_26_19: So is the man that deceiveth his neighbour, and saith, Am not I in sport?
kjv.PRO_26_20: Where no wood is, there the fire goeth out: so where there is no talebearer, the strife ceaseth.
kjv.PRO_26_21: As coals are to burning coals, and wood to fire; so is a contentious man to kindle strife.
kjv.PRO_26_22: The words of a talebearer are as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.
kjv.PRO_26_23: Burning lips and a wicked heart are like a potsherd covered with silver dross.
kjv.PRO_26_24: He that hateth dissembleth with his lips, and layeth up deceit within him;
kjv.PRO_26_25: When he speaketh fair, believe him not: for there are seven abominations in his heart.
kjv.PRO_26_26: Whose hatred is covered by deceit, his wickedness shall be shewed before the whole congregation.
kjv.PRO_26_27: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein: and he that rolleth a stone, it will return upon him.
kjv.PRO_26_28: A lying tongue hateth those that are afflicted by it; and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.
kjv.PRO_27_1: Boast not thyself of to morrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.
kjv.PRO_27_2: Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.
kjv.PRO_27_3: A stone is heavy, and the sand weighty; but a fool's wrath is heavier than them both.
kjv.PRO_27_4: Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?
kjv.PRO_27_5: Open rebuke is better than secret love.
kjv.PRO_27_6: Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful.
kjv.PRO_27_7: The full soul loatheth an honeycomb; but to the hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.
kjv.PRO_27_8: As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so is a man that wandereth from his place.
kjv.PRO_27_9: Ointment and perfume rejoice the heart: so doth the sweetness of a man's friend by hearty counsel.
kjv.PRO_27_10: Thine own friend, and thy father's friend, forsake not; neither go into thy brother's house in the day of thy calamity: for better is a neighbour that is near than a brother far off.
kjv.PRO_27_11: My son, be wise, and make my heart glad, that I may answer him that reproacheth me.
kjv.PRO_27_12: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself; but the simple pass on, and are punished.
kjv.PRO_27_13: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger, and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.
kjv.PRO_27_14: He that blesseth his friend with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, it shall be counted a curse to him.
kjv.PRO_27_15: A continual dropping in a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike.
kjv.PRO_27_16: Whosoever hideth her hideth the wind, and the ointment of his right hand, which bewrayeth itself.
kjv.PRO_27_17: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.
kjv.PRO_27_18: Whoso keepeth the fig tree shall eat the fruit thereof: so he that waiteth on his master shall be honoured.
kjv.PRO_27_19: As in water face answereth to face, so the heart of man to man.
kjv.PRO_27_20: Hell and destruction are never full; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.
kjv.PRO_27_21: As the fining pot for silver, and the furnace for gold; so is a man to his praise.
kjv.PRO_27_22: Though thou shouldest bray a fool in a mortar among wheat with a pestle, yet will not his foolishness depart from him.
kjv.PRO_27_23: Be thou diligent to know the state of thy flocks, and look well to thy herds.
kjv.PRO_27_24: For riches are not for ever: and doth the crown endure to every generation?
kjv.PRO_27_25: The hay appeareth, and the tender grass sheweth itself, and herbs of the mountains are gathered.
kjv.PRO_27_26: The lambs are for thy clothing, and the goats are the price of the field.
kjv.PRO_27_27: And thou shalt have goats' milk enough for thy food, for the food of thy household, and for the maintenance for thy maidens.
kjv.PRO_28_1: The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion.
kjv.PRO_28_2: For the transgression of a land many are the princes thereof: but by a man of understanding and knowledge the state thereof shall be prolonged.
kjv.PRO_28_3: A poor man that oppresseth the poor is like a sweeping rain which leaveth no food.
kjv.PRO_28_4: They that forsake the law praise the wicked: but such as keep the law contend with them.
kjv.PRO_28_5: Evil men understand not judgment: but they that seek the Lord understand all things.
kjv.PRO_28_6: Better is the poor that walketh in his uprightness, than he that is perverse in his ways, though he be rich.
kjv.PRO_28_7: Whoso keepeth the law is a wise son: but he that is a companion of riotous men shameth his father.
kjv.PRO_28_8: He that by usury and unjust gain increaseth his substance, he shall gather it for him that will pity the poor.
kjv.PRO_28_9: He that turneth away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer shall be abomination.
kjv.PRO_28_10: Whoso causeth the righteous to go astray in an evil way, he shall fall himself into his own pit: but the upright shall have good things in possession.
kjv.PRO_28_11: The rich man is wise in his own conceit; but the poor that hath understanding searcheth him out.
kjv.PRO_28_12: When righteous men do rejoice, there is great glory: but when the wicked rise, a man is hidden.
kjv.PRO_28_13: He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy.
kjv.PRO_28_14: Happy is the man that feareth alway: but he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into mischief.
kjv.PRO_28_15: As a roaring lion, and a ranging bear; so is a wicked ruler over the poor people.
kjv.PRO_28_16: The prince that wanteth understanding is also a great oppressor: but he that hateth covetousness shall prolong his days.
kjv.PRO_28_17: A man that doeth violence to the blood of any person shall flee to the pit; let no man stay him.
kjv.PRO_28_18: Whoso walketh uprightly shall be saved: but he that is perverse in his ways shall fall at once.
kjv.PRO_28_19: He that tilleth his land shall have plenty of bread: but he that followeth after vain persons shall have poverty enough.
kjv.PRO_28_20: A faithful man shall abound with blessings: but he that maketh haste to be rich shall not be innocent.
kjv.PRO_28_21: To have respect of persons is not good: for for a piece of bread that man will transgress.
kjv.PRO_28_22: He that hasteth to be rich hath an evil eye, and considereth not that poverty shall come upon him.
kjv.PRO_28_23: He that rebuketh a man afterwards shall find more favour than he that flattereth with the tongue.
kjv.PRO_28_24: Whoso robbeth his father or his mother, and saith, It is no transgression; the same is the companion of a destroyer.
kjv.PRO_28_25: He that is of a proud heart stirreth up strife: but he that putteth his trust in the Lord shall be made fat.
kjv.PRO_28_26: He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered.
kjv.PRO_28_27: He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack: but he that hideth his eyes shall have many a curse.
kjv.PRO_28_28: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves: but when they perish, the righteous increase.
kjv.PRO_29_1: He, that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.
kjv.PRO_29_2: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn.
kjv.PRO_29_3: Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father: but he that keepeth company with harlots spendeth his substance.
kjv.PRO_29_4: The king by judgment establisheth the land: but he that receiveth gifts overthroweth it.
kjv.PRO_29_5: A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his feet.
kjv.PRO_29_6: In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.
kjv.PRO_29_7: The righteous considereth the cause of the poor: but the wicked regardeth not to know it.
kjv.PRO_29_8: Scornful men bring a city into a snare: but wise men turn away wrath.
kjv.PRO_29_9: If a wise man contendeth with a foolish man, whether he rage or laugh, there is no rest.
kjv.PRO_29_10: The bloodthirsty hate the upright: but the just seek his soul.
kjv.PRO_29_11: A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.
kjv.PRO_29_12: If a ruler hearken to lies, all his servants are wicked.
kjv.PRO_29_13: The poor and the deceitful man meet together: the Lord lighteneth both their eyes.
kjv.PRO_29_14: The king that faithfully judgeth the poor, his throne shall be established for ever.
kjv.PRO_29_15: The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame.
kjv.PRO_29_16: When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increaseth: but the righteous shall see their fall.
kjv.PRO_29_17: Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.
kjv.PRO_29_18: Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.
kjv.PRO_29_19: A servant will not be corrected by words: for though he understand he will not answer.
kjv.PRO_29_20: Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
kjv.PRO_29_21: He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.
kjv.PRO_29_22: An angry man stirreth up strife, and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.
kjv.PRO_29_23: A man's pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.
kjv.PRO_29_24: Whoso is partner with a thief hateth his own soul: he heareth cursing, and bewrayeth it not.
kjv.PRO_29_25: The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the Lord shall be safe.
kjv.PRO_29_26: Many seek the ruler's favour; but every man's judgment cometh from the Lord.
kjv.PRO_29_27: An unjust man is an abomination to the just: and he that is upright in the way is abomination to the wicked.
kjv.PRO_30_1: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, even the prophecy: the man spake unto Ithiel, even unto Ithiel and Ucal,
kjv.PRO_30_2: Surely I am more brutish than any man, and have not the understanding of a man.
kjv.PRO_30_3: I neither learned wisdom, nor have the knowledge of the holy.
kjv.PRO_30_4: Who hath ascended up into heaven, or descended? who hath gathered the wind in his fists? who hath bound the waters in a garment? who hath established all the ends of the earth? what is his name, and what is his son's name, if thou canst tell?
kjv.PRO_30_5: Every word of God is pure: he is a shield unto them that put their trust in him.
kjv.PRO_30_6: Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee, and thou be found a liar.
kjv.PRO_30_7: Two things have I required of thee; deny me them not before I die:
kjv.PRO_30_8: Remove far from me vanity and lies: give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me:
kjv.PRO_30_9: Lest I be full, and deny thee, and say, Who is the Lord? or lest I be poor, and steal, and take the name of my God in vain.
kjv.PRO_30_10: Accuse not a servant unto his master, lest he curse thee, and thou be found guilty.
kjv.PRO_30_11: There is a generation that curseth their father, and doth not bless their mother.
kjv.PRO_30_12: There is a generation that are pure in their own eyes, and yet is not washed from their filthiness.
kjv.PRO_30_13: There is a generation, O how lofty are their eyes! and their eyelids are lifted up.
kjv.PRO_30_14: There is a generation, whose teeth are as swords, and their jaw teeth as knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, and the needy from among men.
kjv.PRO_30_15: The horseleach hath two daughters, crying, Give, give. There are three things that are never satisfied, yea, four things say not, It is enough:
kjv.PRO_30_16: The grave; and the barren womb; the earth that is not filled with water; and the fire that saith not, It is enough.
kjv.PRO_30_17: The eye that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young eagles shall eat it.
kjv.PRO_30_18: There be three things which are too wonderful for me, yea, four which I know not:
kjv.PRO_30_19: The way of an eagle in the air; the way of a serpent upon a rock; the way of a ship in the midst of the sea; and the way of a man with a maid.
kjv.PRO_30_20: Such is the way of an adulterous woman; she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I have done no wickedness.
kjv.PRO_30_21: For three things the earth is disquieted, and for four which it cannot bear:
kjv.PRO_30_22: For a servant when he reigneth; and a fool when he is filled with meat;
kjv.PRO_30_23: For an odious woman when she is married; and an handmaid that is heir to her mistress.
kjv.PRO_30_24: There be four things which are little upon the earth, but they are exceeding wise:
kjv.PRO_30_25: The ants are a people not strong, yet they prepare their meat in the summer;
kjv.PRO_30_26: The conies are but a feeble folk, yet make they their houses in the rocks;
kjv.PRO_30_27: The locusts have no king, yet go they forth all of them by bands;
kjv.PRO_30_28: The spider taketh hold with her hands, and is in kings' palaces.
kjv.PRO_30_29: There be three things which go well, yea, four are comely in going:
kjv.PRO_30_30: A lion which is strongest among beasts, and turneth not away for any;
kjv.PRO_30_31: A greyhound; an he goat also; and a king, against whom there is no rising up.
kjv.PRO_30_32: If thou hast done foolishly in lifting up thyself, or if thou hast thought evil, lay thine hand upon thy mouth.
kjv.PRO_30_33: Surely the churning of milk bringeth forth butter, and the wringing of the nose bringeth forth blood: so the forcing of wrath bringeth forth strife.
kjv.PRO_31_1: The words of king Lemuel, the prophecy that his mother taught him.
kjv.PRO_31_2: What, my son? and what, the son of my womb? and what, the son of my vows?
kjv.PRO_31_3: Give not thy strength unto women, nor thy ways to that which destroyeth kings.
kjv.PRO_31_4: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine; nor for princes strong drink:
kjv.PRO_31_5: Lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the judgment of any of the afflicted.
kjv.PRO_31_6: Give strong drink unto him that is ready to perish, and wine unto those that be of heavy hearts.
kjv.PRO_31_7: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.
kjv.PRO_31_8: Open thy mouth for the dumb in the cause of all such as are appointed to destruction.
kjv.PRO_31_9: Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
kjv.PRO_31_10: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.
kjv.PRO_31_11: The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil.
kjv.PRO_31_12: She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life.
kjv.PRO_31_13: She seeketh wool, and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands.
kjv.PRO_31_14: She is like the merchants' ships; she bringeth her food from afar.
kjv.PRO_31_15: She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and a portion to her maidens.
kjv.PRO_31_16: She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard.
kjv.PRO_31_17: She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms.
kjv.PRO_31_18: She perceiveth that her merchandise is good: her candle goeth not out by night.
kjv.PRO_31_19: She layeth her hands to the spindle, and her hands hold the distaff.
kjv.PRO_31_20: She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy.
kjv.PRO_31_21: She is not afraid of the snow for her household: for all her household are clothed with scarlet.
kjv.PRO_31_22: She maketh herself coverings of tapestry; her clothing is silk and purple.
kjv.PRO_31_23: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land.
kjv.PRO_31_24: She maketh fine linen, and selleth it; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.
kjv.PRO_31_25: Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.
kjv.PRO_31_26: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness.
kjv.PRO_31_27: She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.
kjv.PRO_31_28: Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her.
kjv.PRO_31_29: Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all.
kjv.PRO_31_30: Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the Lord, she shall be praised.
kjv.PRO_31_31: Give her of the fruit of her hands; and let her own works praise her in the gates.

net.PRO_1_1: The proverbs of Solomon, son of David, king of Israel:
net.PRO_1_2: To learn wisdom and moral instruction, to discern wise counsel.
net.PRO_1_3: To receive moral instruction in skillful living, with righteousness, justice, and equity.
net.PRO_1_4: To impart shrewdness to the morally naive, a discerning plan to the young person.
net.PRO_1_5: (Let the wise also hear and gain instruction, and let the discerning acquire guidance!)
net.PRO_1_6: To discern the meaning of a proverb and a parable, the sayings of the wise and their riddles. Introduction to the Theme of the Book
net.PRO_1_7: Fearing the Lord is the beginning of discernment, but fools have despised wisdom and moral instruction.
net.PRO_1_8: Listen, my child, to the instruction from your father, and do not forsake the teaching from your mother.
net.PRO_1_9: For they will be like an elegant garland on your head, and like pendants around your neck. Admonition to Avoid Easy but Unjust Riches
net.PRO_1_10: My child, if sinners try to entice you, do not consent!
net.PRO_1_11: If they say, “Come with us! We will lie in wait to shed blood; we will ambush an innocent person capriciously.
net.PRO_1_12: We will swallow them alive like Sheol, those full of vigor like those going down to the Pit.
net.PRO_1_13: We will seize all kinds of precious wealth; we will fill our houses with plunder.
net.PRO_1_14: Join with us! We will all share equally in what we steal.”
net.PRO_1_15: My child, do not go down their way, withhold yourself from their path;
net.PRO_1_16: for they are eager to inflict harm, and they hasten to shed blood.
net.PRO_1_17: Surely it is futile to spread a net in plain sight of any bird,
net.PRO_1_18: but these men lie in wait for their own blood, they ambush their own lives!
net.PRO_1_19: Such are the ways of all who gain profit unjustly; it takes away the life of those who obtain it! Warning Against Disregarding Wisdom
net.PRO_1_20: Wisdom calls out in the street, she shouts loudly in the plazas;
net.PRO_1_21: at the head of the noisy streets she calls, in the entrances of the gates in the city she utters her words:
net.PRO_1_22: “How long will you simpletons love naiveté? How long have mockers delighted in mockery? And how long will fools hate knowledge?
net.PRO_1_23: You should respond to my rebuke. Then I would pour out my thoughts to you; I would make my words known to you.
net.PRO_1_24: However, because I called but you refused to listen, because I stretched out my hand but no one was paying attention,
net.PRO_1_25: and you neglected all my advice, and did not comply with my rebuke,
net.PRO_1_26: so I myself will laugh when disaster strikes you, I will mock when what you dread comes,
net.PRO_1_27: when what you dread comes like a whirlwind, and disaster strikes you like a devastating storm, when distressing trouble comes on you.
net.PRO_1_28: Then they will call to me, but I will not answer; they will diligently seek me, but they will not find me.
net.PRO_1_29: Because they hated moral knowledge, and did not choose to fear the Lord,
net.PRO_1_30: they did not comply with my advice, they spurned all my rebuke.
net.PRO_1_31: Therefore they will eat from the fruit of their way, and they will be stuffed full of their own counsel.
net.PRO_1_32: For the waywardness of the simpletons will kill them, and the careless ease of fools will destroy them.
net.PRO_1_33: But the one who listens to me will live in security, and will be at ease from the dread of harm.”
net.PRO_2_1: My child, if you receive my words, and store up my commands inside yourself,
net.PRO_2_2: by making your ear attentive to wisdom, and by turning your heart to understanding,
net.PRO_2_3: indeed, if you call out for discernment — shout loudly for understanding—
net.PRO_2_4: if you seek it like silver, and search for it like hidden treasure,
net.PRO_2_5: then you will understand how to fear the Lord, and you will discover knowledge about God.
net.PRO_2_6: For the Lord gives wisdom, and from his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.
net.PRO_2_7: He stores up effective counsel for the upright, and is like a shield for those who live with integrity,
net.PRO_2_8: to guard the paths of the righteous and to protect the way of his pious ones.
net.PRO_2_9: Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity—every good way.
net.PRO_2_10: For wisdom will enter your heart, and moral knowledge will be attractive to you.
net.PRO_2_11: Discretion will protect you, understanding will guard you,
net.PRO_2_12: to deliver you from the way of the wicked, from those speaking perversity,
net.PRO_2_13: who leave the upright paths to walk on the dark ways,
net.PRO_2_14: who delight in doing evil, they rejoice in perverse evil;
net.PRO_2_15: whose paths are morally crooked, and who are devious in their ways;
net.PRO_2_16: to deliver you from the adulterous woman, from the loose woman who has flattered you with her words;
net.PRO_2_17: who leaves the husband from her younger days, and has ignored her marriage covenant made before God.
net.PRO_2_18: For she has set her house by death, and her paths by the place of the departed spirits.
net.PRO_2_19: None who go in to her will return, nor will they reach the paths of life.
net.PRO_2_20: So you will walk in the way of good people, and will keep on the paths of the righteous.
net.PRO_2_21: For the upright will reside in the land, and those with integrity will remain in it,
net.PRO_2_22: but the wicked will be removed from the land, and the treacherous will be torn away from it.
net.PRO_3_1: My child, do not forget my teaching, but let your heart keep my commandments,
net.PRO_3_2: for they will provide a long and full life, and well-being for you.
net.PRO_3_3: Do not let mercy and truth leave you; bind them around your neck, write them on the tablet of your heart.
net.PRO_3_4: Then you will find favor and good understanding, in the sight of God and people.
net.PRO_3_5: Trust in the Lord with all your heart, and do not rely on your own understanding.
net.PRO_3_6: Acknowledge him in all your ways, and he will make your paths straight.
net.PRO_3_7: Do not be wise in your own estimation; fear the Lord and turn away from evil.
net.PRO_3_8: This will bring healing to your body, and refreshment to your inner self.
net.PRO_3_9: Honor the Lord from your wealth and from the firstfruits of all your crops;
net.PRO_3_10: then your barns will be filled completely, and your vats will overflow with new wine.
net.PRO_3_11: My child, do not despise discipline from the Lord, and do not loathe his rebuke.
net.PRO_3_12: For the Lord disciplines those he loves, just as a father disciplines the son in whom he delights. Blessings of Obtaining Wisdom
net.PRO_3_13: Blessed is the one who has found wisdom, and the one who obtains understanding.
net.PRO_3_14: For her benefit is more profitable than silver, and her gain is better than gold.
net.PRO_3_15: She is more precious than rubies, and none of the things you desire can compare with her.
net.PRO_3_16: Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
net.PRO_3_17: Her ways are very pleasant, and all her paths are peaceful.
net.PRO_3_18: She is like a tree of life to those who grasp onto her, and everyone who takes hold of her will be blessed.
net.PRO_3_19: By wisdom the Lord laid the foundation of the earth; he established the heavens by understanding.
net.PRO_3_20: By his knowledge the primordial sea was broken open, so that the clouds drip down dew.
net.PRO_3_21: My child, do not let them escape from your sight; safeguard sound wisdom and discretion.
net.PRO_3_22: So they will become life for your soul, and grace around your neck.
net.PRO_3_23: Then you will walk on your way with security, and you will not stumble.
net.PRO_3_24: When you lie down you will not be filled with fear; when you lie down your sleep will be pleasant.
net.PRO_3_25: Do not be afraid of sudden disaster, or when destruction overtakes the wicked;
net.PRO_3_26: for the Lord will be the source of your confidence, and he will guard your foot from being caught in a trap. Wisdom Demonstrated in Relationships with People
net.PRO_3_27: Do not withhold good from those who need it, when you have the ability to help.
net.PRO_3_28: Do not say to your neighbor, “Go! Return tomorrow and I will give it,” when you have it with you at the time.
net.PRO_3_29: Do not plot evil against your neighbor when he dwells by you unsuspectingly.
net.PRO_3_30: Do not accuse anyone without legitimate cause, if he has not treated you wrongly.
net.PRO_3_31: Do not envy a violent man, and do not choose any of his ways;
net.PRO_3_32: for one who goes astray is an abomination to the Lord, but he reveals his intimate counsel to the upright.
net.PRO_3_33: The Lord’s curse is on the household of the wicked, but he blesses the home of the righteous.
net.PRO_3_34: With arrogant scoffers he is scornful, yet he shows favor to the humble.
net.PRO_3_35: The wise inherit honor, but he holds fools up to public contempt.
net.PRO_4_1: Listen, children, to a father’s instruction, and pay attention so that you may gain discernment.
net.PRO_4_2: Because I hereby give you good instruction, do not forsake my teaching.
net.PRO_4_3: When I was a son to my father, a tender, only child before my mother,
net.PRO_4_4: he taught me, and he said to me: “Let your heart lay hold of my words; keep my commands so that you will live.
net.PRO_4_5: Acquire wisdom, acquire understanding; do not forget and do not turn aside from the words I speak.
net.PRO_4_6: Do not forsake wisdom, and she will protect you; love her, and she will guard you.
net.PRO_4_7: Wisdom is supreme —so acquire wisdom, and whatever you acquire, acquire understanding!
net.PRO_4_8: Esteem her highly and she will exalt you; she will honor you if you embrace her.
net.PRO_4_9: She will place a fair garland on your head; she will bestow a beautiful crown on you.”
net.PRO_4_10: Listen, my child, and accept my words, so that the years of your life will be many.
net.PRO_4_11: I hereby guide you in the way of wisdom and I lead you in upright paths.
net.PRO_4_12: When you walk, your steps will not be hampered, and when you run, you will not stumble.
net.PRO_4_13: Hold on to instruction, do not let it go; protect it, because it is your life.
net.PRO_4_14: Do not enter the path of the wicked or walk in the way of those who are evil.
net.PRO_4_15: Avoid it, do not go on it; turn away from it, and go on.
net.PRO_4_16: For they cannot sleep unless they cause harm; they are robbed of sleep until they make someone stumble.
net.PRO_4_17: Indeed they have eaten bread gained from wickedness and drink wine obtained from violence.
net.PRO_4_18: But the path of the righteous is like the bright morning light, growing brighter and brighter until full day.
net.PRO_4_19: The way of the wicked is like gloomy darkness; they do not know what they stumble over.
net.PRO_4_20: My child, pay attention to my words; listen attentively to my sayings.
net.PRO_4_21: Do not let them depart from your sight, guard them within your heart;
net.PRO_4_22: for they are life to those who find them and healing to one’s entire body.
net.PRO_4_23: Guard your heart with all vigilance, for from it are the sources of life.
net.PRO_4_24: Remove perverse speech from your mouth; keep devious talk far from your lips.
net.PRO_4_25: Let your eyes look directly in front of you and let your gaze look straight before you.
net.PRO_4_26: Make the path for your feet level, so that all your ways may be established.
net.PRO_4_27: Do not turn to the right or to the left; turn yourself away from evil.
net.PRO_5_1: My child, be attentive to my wisdom, pay close attention to my understanding,
net.PRO_5_2: in order to safeguard discretion, and that your lips may guard knowledge.
net.PRO_5_3: For the lips of the adulterous woman drip honey, and her seductive words are smoother than olive oil,
net.PRO_5_4: but in the end she is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword.
net.PRO_5_5: Her feet go down to death; her steps lead straight to the grave.
net.PRO_5_6: Lest she should make level the path leading to life, her paths have wandered, but she is not able to discern it.
net.PRO_5_7: So now, children, listen to me; do not turn aside from the words I speak.
net.PRO_5_8: Keep yourself far from her, and do not go near the door of her house,
net.PRO_5_9: lest you give your vigor to others and your years to a cruel person,
net.PRO_5_10: lest strangers devour your strength, and your labor benefit another man’s house.
net.PRO_5_11: And at the end of your life you will groan when your flesh and your body are wasted away.
net.PRO_5_12: And you will say, “How I hated discipline! My heart spurned reproof!
net.PRO_5_13: For I did not obey my teachers and I did not heed my instructors.
net.PRO_5_14: I almost came to complete ruin in the midst of the whole congregation!”
net.PRO_5_15: Drink water from your own cistern and running water from your own well.
net.PRO_5_16: Should your springs be dispersed outside, your streams of water in the wide plazas?
net.PRO_5_17: Let them be for yourself alone, and not for strangers with you.
net.PRO_5_18: May your fountain be blessed, and may you rejoice in the wife you married in your youth —
net.PRO_5_19: a loving doe, a graceful deer; may her breasts satisfy you at all times, may you be captivated by her love always.
net.PRO_5_20: But why should you be captivated, my son, by an adulteress, and embrace the bosom of a different woman?
net.PRO_5_21: For the ways of a person are in front of the Lord’s eyes, and the Lord weighs all that person’s paths.
net.PRO_5_22: The wicked will be captured by his own iniquities, and he will be held by the cords of his own sin.
net.PRO_5_23: He will die because there was no discipline; because of the greatness of his folly he will reel.
net.PRO_6_1: My child, if you have made a pledge for your neighbor, if you have become a guarantor for a stranger,
net.PRO_6_2: if you have been ensnared by the words you have uttered, and have been caught by the words you have spoken,
net.PRO_6_3: then, my child, do this in order to deliver yourself, because you have fallen into your neighbor’s power: Go, humble yourself, and appeal firmly to your neighbor.
net.PRO_6_4: Permit no sleep to your eyes or slumber to your eyelids.
net.PRO_6_5: Deliver yourself like a gazelle from a snare, and like a bird from the trap of the fowler.
net.PRO_6_6: Go to the ant, you sluggard; observe her ways and be wise!
net.PRO_6_7: It has no commander, overseer, or ruler,
net.PRO_6_8: yet it would prepare its food in the summer; it gathered at the harvest what it will eat.
net.PRO_6_9: How long, you sluggard, will you lie there? When will you rise from your sleep?
net.PRO_6_10: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to relax,
net.PRO_6_11: and your poverty will come like a robber, and your need like an armed man.
net.PRO_6_12: A worthless and wicked person walks around saying perverse things;
net.PRO_6_13: he winks with his eyes, signals with his feet, and points with his fingers;
net.PRO_6_14: he plots evil with perverse thoughts in his heart, he spreads contention at all times.
net.PRO_6_15: Therefore, his disaster will come suddenly; in an instant he will be broken, and there will be no remedy.
net.PRO_6_16: There are six things that the Lord hates, even seven things that are an abomination to him:
net.PRO_6_17: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,
net.PRO_6_18: a heart that devises wicked plans, feet that are swift to run to evil,
net.PRO_6_19: a false witness who pours out lies, and a person who spreads discord among family members.
net.PRO_6_20: My child, guard the commands of your father and do not forsake the instruction of your mother.
net.PRO_6_21: Bind them on your heart continually; fasten them around your neck.
net.PRO_6_22: When you walk about, they will guide you; when you lie down, they will watch over you; when you wake up, they will talk to you.
net.PRO_6_23: For the commandments are like a lamp, instruction is like a light, and rebukes of discipline are like the road leading to life,
net.PRO_6_24: by keeping you from the evil woman, from the smooth tongue of the loose woman.
net.PRO_6_25: Do not lust in your heart for her beauty, and do not let her captivate you with her alluring eyes;
net.PRO_6_26: for on account of a prostitute one is brought down to a loaf of bread, but the wife of another man preys on your precious life.
net.PRO_6_27: Can a man hold fire against his chest without burning his clothes?
net.PRO_6_28: Can a man walk on hot coals without scorching his feet?
net.PRO_6_29: So it is with the one who sleeps with his neighbor’s wife; no one who touches her will escape punishment.
net.PRO_6_30: People do not despise a thief when he steals to fulfill his need when he is hungry.
net.PRO_6_31: Yet if he is caught he must repay seven times over, he might even have to give all the wealth of his house.
net.PRO_6_32: A man who commits adultery with a woman lacks sense, whoever does it destroys his own life.
net.PRO_6_33: He will be beaten and despised, and his reproach will not be wiped away;
net.PRO_6_34: for jealousy kindles a husband’s rage, and he will not show mercy when he takes revenge.
net.PRO_6_35: He will not consider any compensation; he will not be willing, even if you multiply the compensation.
net.PRO_7_1: My child, devote yourself to my words and store up my commands inside yourself.
net.PRO_7_2: Keep my commands so that you may live, and obey my instruction as your most prized possession.
net.PRO_7_3: Bind them on your forearm; write them on the tablet of your heart.
net.PRO_7_4: Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” and call understanding a close relative,
net.PRO_7_5: so that they may keep you from the adulterous woman, from the loose woman who has flattered you with her words.
net.PRO_7_6: For at the window of my house through my window lattice I looked out
net.PRO_7_7: and I saw among the naive — I discerned among the youths — a young man who lacked sense.
net.PRO_7_8: He was passing by the street near her corner, making his way along the road to her house
net.PRO_7_9: in the twilight, the evening, in the dark of the night.
net.PRO_7_10: Suddenly a woman came out to meet him! She was dressed like a prostitute and with secret intent.
net.PRO_7_11: (She is loud and rebellious, she does not remain at home—
net.PRO_7_12: at one time outside, at another in the wide plazas, and by every corner she lies in wait.)
net.PRO_7_13: So she grabbed him and kissed him, and with a bold expression she said to him,
net.PRO_7_14: “I have meat from my peace offerings at home; today I have fulfilled my vows!
net.PRO_7_15: That is why I came out to meet you, to look for you, and I found you!
net.PRO_7_16: I have spread my bed with elegant coverings, with richly colored fabric from Egypt.
net.PRO_7_17: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
net.PRO_7_18: Come, let’s drink deeply of lovemaking until morning, let’s delight ourselves with love’s pleasures.
net.PRO_7_19: For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a journey of some distance.
net.PRO_7_20: He has taken a bag of money with him; he will not return until the end of the month.”
net.PRO_7_21: She turned him aside with her persuasions; with her smooth talk she was enticing him along.
net.PRO_7_22: Suddenly he was going after her like an ox that goes to the slaughter, like a stag prancing into a trapper’s snare
net.PRO_7_23: till an arrow pierces his liver — like a bird hurrying into a trap, and he does not know that it will cost him his life.
net.PRO_7_24: So now, sons, listen to me, and pay attention to the words I speak.
net.PRO_7_25: Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways— do not wander into her pathways;
net.PRO_7_26: for she has brought down many fatally wounded, and all those she has slain are many.
net.PRO_7_27: Her house is the way to the grave, going down to the chambers of death.
net.PRO_8_1: Does not wisdom call out? Does not understanding raise her voice?
net.PRO_8_2: At the top of the prominent places along the way, at the intersection of the paths she has taken her stand;
net.PRO_8_3: beside the gates opening into the city, at the entrance of the doorways she cries out:
net.PRO_8_4: “To you, O people, I call out, and my voice calls to all mankind.
net.PRO_8_5: You who are naive, discern wisdom! And you fools, understand discernment!
net.PRO_8_6: Listen, for I will speak excellent things, and my lips will utter what is right.
net.PRO_8_7: For my mouth speaks truth, and my lips hate wickedness.
net.PRO_8_8: All the words of my mouth are righteous; there is nothing in them twisted or crooked.
net.PRO_8_9: All of them are clear to the discerning and upright to those who find knowledge.
net.PRO_8_10: Receive my instruction rather than silver, and knowledge rather than choice gold.
net.PRO_8_11: For wisdom is better than rubies, and desirable things cannot be compared to her.
net.PRO_8_12: “I, wisdom, have dwelt with prudence, and I find knowledge and discretion.
net.PRO_8_13: The fear of the Lord is to hate evil; I hate arrogant pride and the evil way and perverse utterances.
net.PRO_8_14: Counsel and sound wisdom belong to me; I possess understanding and might.
net.PRO_8_15: By me kings reign, and by me potentates decree righteousness;
net.PRO_8_16: by me princes rule, as well as nobles and all righteous judges.
net.PRO_8_17: I will love those who love me, and those who seek me diligently will find me.
net.PRO_8_18: Riches and honor are with me, long-lasting wealth and righteousness.
net.PRO_8_19: My fruit is better than the purest gold, and my harvest is better than choice silver.
net.PRO_8_20: I walk in the path of righteousness, in the pathway of justice,
net.PRO_8_21: that I may cause those who love me to inherit wealth, and that I may fill their treasuries.
net.PRO_8_22: The Lord created me as the beginning of his works, before his deeds of long ago.
net.PRO_8_23: From eternity I have been fashioned, from the beginning, from before the world existed.
net.PRO_8_24: When there were no deep oceans I was born, when there were no springs overflowing with water;
net.PRO_8_25: before the mountains were set in place— before the hills—I was born,
net.PRO_8_26: before he made the earth and its fields, or the top soil of the world.
net.PRO_8_27: When he established the heavens, I was there; when he marked out the horizon over the face of the deep,
net.PRO_8_28: when he established the clouds above, when he secured the fountains of the deep,
net.PRO_8_29: when he gave the sea his decree that the waters should not pass over his command, when he marked out the foundations of the earth,
net.PRO_8_30: then I was beside him as a master craftsman, and I was his delight day by day, rejoicing before him at all times,
net.PRO_8_31: rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth, and delighting in its people.
net.PRO_8_32: “So now, children, listen to me; blessed are those who keep my ways.
net.PRO_8_33: Listen to my instruction so that you may be wise, and do not neglect it.
net.PRO_8_34: Blessed is the one who listens to me, watching at my doors day by day, waiting beside my doorway.
net.PRO_8_35: For the one who finds me has found life and received favor from the Lord.
net.PRO_8_36: But the one who misses me brings harm to himself; all who hate me love death.”
net.PRO_9_1: Wisdom has built her house; she has carved out its seven pillars.
net.PRO_9_2: She has prepared her meat, she has mixed her wine; she also has arranged her table.
net.PRO_9_3: She has sent out her female servants; she calls out on the highest places of the city.
net.PRO_9_4: “Whoever is naive, let him turn in here.” To those who lack understanding, she has said,
net.PRO_9_5: “Come, eat some of my food, and drink some of the wine I have mixed.
net.PRO_9_6: Abandon your foolish ways so that you may live, and proceed in the way of understanding.”
net.PRO_9_7: Whoever corrects a mocker is asking for insult; whoever reproves a wicked person receives abuse.
net.PRO_9_8: Do not reprove a mocker or he will hate you; reprove a wise person and he will love you.
net.PRO_9_9: Give instruction to a wise person, and he will become wiser still; teach a righteous person and he will add to his learning.
net.PRO_9_10: The beginning of wisdom is to fear the Lord, and acknowledging the Holy One is understanding.
net.PRO_9_11: For because of me your days will be many, and years will be added to your life.
net.PRO_9_12: If you are wise, you are wise to your own advantage, but if you have mocked, you alone must bear it.
net.PRO_9_13: The woman called Folly is brash, she is naive and does not know anything.
net.PRO_9_14: And she has sat down at the door of her house, on a seat at the highest point of the city,
net.PRO_9_15: calling out to those who are passing by her in the way, who go straight on their way.
net.PRO_9_16: “Whoever is naive, let him turn in here,” To those who lack understanding she has said,
net.PRO_9_17: “Stolen waters are sweet, and food obtained in secret is pleasant!”
net.PRO_9_18: But they do not realize that the dead are there, that her guests are in the depths of the grave.
net.PRO_10_1: The proverbs of Solomon: A wise child makes a father rejoice, but a foolish child is a grief to his mother.
net.PRO_10_2: Treasures gained by wickedness do not profit, but righteousness delivers from death.
net.PRO_10_3: The Lord satisfies the appetite of the righteous, but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.
net.PRO_10_4: The one who is lazy becomes poor, but the one who works diligently becomes wealthy.
net.PRO_10_5: The one who gathers crops in the summer is a wise son, but the one who sleeps during harvest is a shameful son.
net.PRO_10_6: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but the speech of the wicked conceals violence.
net.PRO_10_7: The memory of the righteous is a blessing, but the reputation of the wicked will rot.
net.PRO_10_8: The wise person accepts instructions, but the one who speaks foolishness will come to ruin.
net.PRO_10_9: The one who conducts himself in integrity will live securely, but the one who behaves perversely will be found out.
net.PRO_10_10: The one who winks his eye causes trouble, and the one who speaks foolishness will come to ruin.
net.PRO_10_11: The speech of the righteous is a fountain of life, but the speech of the wicked conceals violence.
net.PRO_10_12: Hatred stirs up dissension, but love covers all transgressions.
net.PRO_10_13: Wisdom is found in the words of the discerning person, but the one who lacks sense will be disciplined.
net.PRO_10_14: Those who are wise store up knowledge, but foolish speech leads to imminent destruction.
net.PRO_10_15: The wealth of a rich person is like a fortified city, but the poor are brought to ruin by their poverty.
net.PRO_10_16: The reward that the righteous receive is life; the recompense that the wicked receive is judgment.
net.PRO_10_17: The one who heeds instruction is on the way to life, but the one who rejects rebuke goes astray.
net.PRO_10_18: The one who conceals hatred utters lies, and the one who spreads slander is certainly a fool.
net.PRO_10_19: When words abound, transgression is inevitable, but the one who restrains his words is wise.
net.PRO_10_20: What the righteous say is like the best silver, but what the wicked think is of little value.
net.PRO_10_21: The teaching of the righteous feeds many, but fools die for lack of sense.
net.PRO_10_22: The blessing from the Lord makes a person rich, and he adds no sorrow to it.
net.PRO_10_23: Carrying out a wicked scheme is enjoyable to a fool, and so is wisdom for the one who has discernment.
net.PRO_10_24: What the wicked fears will come on him; what the righteous desire will be granted.
net.PRO_10_25: When the storm passes through, the wicked are swept away, but the righteous are an everlasting foundation.
net.PRO_10_26: Like vinegar to the teeth and like smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to those who send him.
net.PRO_10_27: Fearing the Lord prolongs life, but the life span of the wicked will be shortened.
net.PRO_10_28: The hope of the righteous is joy, but the expectation of the wicked perishes.
net.PRO_10_29: The way of the Lord is like a stronghold for the upright, but it is destruction to evildoers.
net.PRO_10_30: The righteous will never be moved, but the wicked will not inhabit the land.
net.PRO_10_31: The speech of the righteous bears the fruit of wisdom, but the one who speaks perversion will be destroyed.
net.PRO_10_32: The lips of the righteous know what is pleasing, but the speech of the wicked is perverse.
net.PRO_11_1: The Lord abhors dishonest scales, but an accurate weight is his delight.
net.PRO_11_2: After pride came, disgrace followed; but wisdom came with humility.
net.PRO_11_3: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them.
net.PRO_11_4: Wealth does not profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.
net.PRO_11_5: The righteousness of the blameless will make their way smooth, but the wicked will fall through their own wickedness.
net.PRO_11_6: The righteousness of the upright will deliver them, but the treacherous will be ensnared by their own desires.
net.PRO_11_7: When a wicked person dies, his expectation perishes, and hope based on power has perished.
net.PRO_11_8: A righteous person was delivered out of trouble, then a wicked person took his place.
net.PRO_11_9: With his speech the godless person destroys his neighbor, but by knowledge the righteous will be delivered.
net.PRO_11_10: When the righteous do well, the city rejoices; when the wicked perish, there is joy.
net.PRO_11_11: A city is exalted by the blessing provided from the upright, but it is destroyed by the counsel of the wicked.
net.PRO_11_12: The one who denounces his neighbor lacks sense, but a discerning person keeps silent.
net.PRO_11_13: The one who goes about slandering others reveals secrets, but the one who is trustworthy conceals a matter.
net.PRO_11_14: When there is no guidance a nation falls, but there is success in the abundance of counselors.
net.PRO_11_15: The one who has put up security for a stranger will surely have trouble, but whoever avoids shaking hands is secure.
net.PRO_11_16: A generous woman gains honor, and ruthless men seize wealth.
net.PRO_11_17: A kind person benefits himself, but a cruel person brings himself trouble.
net.PRO_11_18: The wicked person earns deceitful wages, but the one who sows righteousness reaps a genuine reward.
net.PRO_11_19: True righteousness leads to life, but the one who pursues evil pursues it to his own death.
net.PRO_11_20: The Lord abhors those who are perverse in heart, but those who are blameless in their ways are his delight.
net.PRO_11_21: Be assured that the evil person will not be unpunished, but the descendants of the righteous have escaped harm.
net.PRO_11_22: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout is a beautiful woman who rejects discretion.
net.PRO_11_23: The desire of the righteous is only good, but the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
net.PRO_11_24: One person is generous and yet grows more wealthy, but another withholds more than he should and comes to poverty.
net.PRO_11_25: A generous person will be enriched, and the one who provides water for others will himself be satisfied.
net.PRO_11_26: People will curse the one who withholds grain, but they will praise the one who sells it.
net.PRO_11_27: The one who diligently seeks good seeks favor, but the one who searches for evil—it will come to him.
net.PRO_11_28: The one who trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous will flourish like a green leaf.
net.PRO_11_29: The one who troubles his family will inherit nothing, and the fool will be a servant to the wise person.
net.PRO_11_30: The fruit of the righteous is like a tree producing life, and the one who wins souls is wise.
net.PRO_11_31: If the righteous are recompensed on earth, how much more the wicked sinner!
net.PRO_12_1: The one who loves discipline loves knowledge, but the one who hates reproof is stupid.
net.PRO_12_2: A good person obtains favor from the Lord, but the Lord condemns a person with wicked schemes.
net.PRO_12_3: No one can be established through wickedness, but a righteous root cannot be moved.
net.PRO_12_4: A noble wife is the crown of her husband, but the wife who acts shamefully is like rottenness in his bones.
net.PRO_12_5: The plans of the righteous are just; the counsels of the wicked are deceitful.
net.PRO_12_6: The words of the wicked lie in wait to shed innocent blood, but the words of the upright will deliver them.
net.PRO_12_7: The wicked are overthrown and perish, but the righteous household will stand.
net.PRO_12_8: A person will be praised in accordance with his wisdom, but the one with a bewildered mind will be despised.
net.PRO_12_9: Better is a person of humble standing who works for himself, than one who pretends to be somebody important yet has no food.
net.PRO_12_10: A righteous person cares for the life of his animal, but even the most compassionate acts of the wicked are cruel.
net.PRO_12_11: The one who works his field will have plenty of food, but whoever chases daydreams lacks sense.
net.PRO_12_12: The wicked person has desired the stronghold of the wicked, but the root of the righteous will yield fruit.
net.PRO_12_13: The evil person is ensnared by the transgression of his speech, but the righteous person escapes out of trouble.
net.PRO_12_14: A person will be satisfied with good from the fruit of his words, and the work of his hands will be rendered to him.
net.PRO_12_15: The way of a fool is right in his own opinion, but the one who listens to advice is wise.
net.PRO_12_16: A fool’s annoyance is known at once, but the prudent conceals dishonor.
net.PRO_12_17: The faithful witness tells what is right, but a false witness speaks deceit.
net.PRO_12_18: Speaking recklessly is like the thrusts of a sword, but the words of the wise bring healing.
net.PRO_12_19: The one who tells the truth will endure forever, but the one who lies will last only for a moment.
net.PRO_12_20: Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil, but those who promote peace have joy.
net.PRO_12_21: No harm will be directed at the righteous, but the wicked are filled with calamity.
net.PRO_12_22: The Lord abhors a person who lies, but those who deal truthfully are his delight.
net.PRO_12_23: The shrewd person conceals knowledge, but foolish people proclaim folly.
net.PRO_12_24: The diligent person will rule, but the slothful will be put to forced labor.
net.PRO_12_25: Anxiety in a person’s heart weighs him down, but an encouraging word brings him joy.
net.PRO_12_26: The righteous person is cautious in his friendship, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
net.PRO_12_27: The lazy person does not roast his prey, but personal possessions are precious to the diligent.
net.PRO_12_28: In the path of righteousness there is life, but another path leads to death.
net.PRO_13_1: A wise son accepts his father’s discipline, but a scoffer has never listened to rebuke.
net.PRO_13_2: From the fruit of his speech a person eats good things, but the treacherous desire the fruit of violence.
net.PRO_13_3: The one who guards his words guards his life; whoever is talkative will come to ruin.
net.PRO_13_4: The appetite of the sluggard craves but gets nothing, but the desire of the diligent will be abundantly satisfied.
net.PRO_13_5: The righteous person will reject anything false, but the wicked person will act in shameful disgrace.
net.PRO_13_6: Righteousness guards the one who lives with integrity, but wickedness overthrows the sinner.
net.PRO_13_7: There is one who pretends to be rich and yet has nothing; another pretends to be poor and yet possesses great wealth.
net.PRO_13_8: The ransom of a person’s life is his wealth, thus the poor person has never heard a threat.
net.PRO_13_9: The light of the righteous shines brightly, but the lamp of the wicked goes out.
net.PRO_13_10: With pride comes only contention, but wisdom is with the well-advised.
net.PRO_13_11: Wealth gained quickly will dwindle away, but the one who gathers it little by little will become rich.
net.PRO_13_12: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a longing fulfilled is like a tree of life.
net.PRO_13_13: The one who despises instruction will pay the penalty, but whoever esteems direction will be rewarded.
net.PRO_13_14: Instruction from the wise is like a life-giving fountain, to turn a person from deadly snares.
net.PRO_13_15: Keen insight wins favor, but the conduct of the treacherous ends in destruction.
net.PRO_13_16: Every shrewd person acts with knowledge, but a fool displays his folly.
net.PRO_13_17: An unreliable messenger falls into trouble, but a faithful envoy brings healing.
net.PRO_13_18: The one who neglects discipline ends up in poverty and shame, but the one who accepts reproof is honored.
net.PRO_13_19: A desire fulfilled will be sweet to the soul, but fools abhor turning away from evil.
net.PRO_13_20: The one who associates with the wise grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.
net.PRO_13_21: Calamity pursues sinners, but prosperity rewards the righteous.
net.PRO_13_22: A good person leaves an inheritance for his grandchildren, but the wealth of a sinner is stored up for the righteous.
net.PRO_13_23: Abundant food may come from the field of the poor, but it is swept away by injustice.
net.PRO_13_24: The one who spares his rod hates his child, but the one who loves his child is diligent in disciplining him.
net.PRO_13_25: The righteous has enough food to satisfy his appetite, but the belly of the wicked will be empty.
net.PRO_14_1: Every wise woman has built her household, but a foolish woman tears it down with her own hands.
net.PRO_14_2: The one who walks in his uprightness fears the Lord, but the one who is perverted in his ways despises him.
net.PRO_14_3: In the speech of a fool is a rod for his back, but the words of the wise protect them.
net.PRO_14_4: Where there are no oxen, the feeding trough is clean, but an abundant harvest is produced by strong oxen.
net.PRO_14_5: A truthful witness does not lie, but a false witness breathes out lies.
net.PRO_14_6: The scorner sought wisdom—there was none, but understanding was easy for a discerning person.
net.PRO_14_7: Walk abreast with a foolish person, and you do not understand wise counsel.
net.PRO_14_8: The wisdom of the shrewd person is to discern his way, but the folly of fools is deception.
net.PRO_14_9: Fools mock at reparation, but among the upright there is favor.
net.PRO_14_10: The heart knows its own bitterness, and with its joy no one else can share.
net.PRO_14_11: The household of the wicked will be destroyed, but the tent of the upright will flourish.
net.PRO_14_12: There is a way that seems right to a person, but its end is the way that leads to death.
net.PRO_14_13: Even in laughter the heart may ache, and the end of joy may be grief.
net.PRO_14_14: The backslider will be paid back from his own ways, but a good person will be rewarded for his.
net.PRO_14_15: A naive person will believe anything, but the shrewd person discerns his steps.
net.PRO_14_16: A wise person is cautious and turns from evil, but a fool throws off restraint and is overconfident.
net.PRO_14_17: A person who has a quick temper will do foolish things, and a person with crafty schemes will be hated.
net.PRO_14_18: The naive have inherited folly, but the shrewd will be crowned with knowledge.
net.PRO_14_19: Bad people have bowed before good people, and wicked people have bowed at the gates of someone righteous.
net.PRO_14_20: A poor person will be disliked even by his neighbors, but those who love the rich are many.
net.PRO_14_21: The one who despises his neighbor sins, but whoever is kind to the needy is blessed.
net.PRO_14_22: Do not those who devise evil go astray? But those who plan good exhibit faithful covenant love.
net.PRO_14_23: In all hard work there is profit, but merely talking about it only brings poverty.
net.PRO_14_24: The crown of the wise is their riches, but the folly of fools is folly.
net.PRO_14_25: A truthful witness rescues lives, but one who testifies falsely betrays them.
net.PRO_14_26: In the fear of the Lord one has strong confidence, and it will be a refuge for his children.
net.PRO_14_27: The fear of the Lord is like a life-giving fountain, to turn people from deadly snares.
net.PRO_14_28: A king’s glory is the abundance of people, but the lack of subjects is the ruin of a ruler.
net.PRO_14_29: Someone with great understanding is slow to anger, but the one who has a quick temper exalts folly.
net.PRO_14_30: A tranquil spirit revives the body, but envy is rottenness to the bones.
net.PRO_14_31: The one who oppresses the poor has insulted his Creator, but whoever honors him shows favor to the needy.
net.PRO_14_32: An evil person will be thrown down through his wickedness, but a righteous person takes refuge in his integrity.
net.PRO_14_33: Wisdom rests in the heart of the discerning; it is not known in the inner parts of fools.
net.PRO_14_34: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a disgrace to any people.
net.PRO_14_35: The king shows favor to a wise servant, but his wrath falls on one who acts shamefully.
net.PRO_15_1: A gentle response turns away anger, but a harsh word stirs up wrath.
net.PRO_15_2: The tongue of the wise treats knowledge correctly, but the mouth of the fool spouts out folly.
net.PRO_15_3: The eyes of the Lord are in every place, keeping watch on those who are evil and those who are good.
net.PRO_15_4: Speech that heals is like a life-giving tree, but a perverse speech breaks the spirit.
net.PRO_15_5: A fool rejects his father’s discipline, but whoever heeds reproof shows good sense.
net.PRO_15_6: In the house of the righteous is abundant wealth, but the income of the wicked will be ruined.
net.PRO_15_7: The lips of the wise spread knowledge, but not so the heart of fools.
net.PRO_15_8: The Lord abhors the sacrifice of the wicked, but the prayer of the upright pleases him.
net.PRO_15_9: The Lord abhors the way of the wicked, but he will love those who pursue righteousness.
net.PRO_15_10: Severe discipline is for the one who abandons the way; the one who hates reproof will die.
net.PRO_15_11: Death and Destruction are before the Lord— how much more the hearts of humans!
net.PRO_15_12: The scorner will not love one who corrects him; he will not go to the wise.
net.PRO_15_13: A joyful heart makes the face cheerful, but by a painful heart the spirit is broken.
net.PRO_15_14: The discerning mind seeks knowledge, but the mouth of fools feeds on folly.
net.PRO_15_15: All the days of the afflicted are bad, but one with a cheerful heart has a continual feast.
net.PRO_15_16: Better is little with the fear of the Lord than great wealth and turmoil with it.
net.PRO_15_17: Better a meal of vegetables where there is love than a fattened ox where there is hatred.
net.PRO_15_18: A quick-tempered person stirs up dissension, but one who is slow to anger calms a quarrel.
net.PRO_15_19: The way of the sluggard is like a hedge of thorns, but the path of the upright is like a highway.
net.PRO_15_20: A wise child brings joy to his father, but a foolish person despises his mother.
net.PRO_15_21: Folly is a joy to one who lacks sense, but one who has understanding follows an upright course.
net.PRO_15_22: Plans fail when there is no counsel, but with abundant advisers they are established.
net.PRO_15_23: A person has joy in giving an appropriate answer, and a word at the right time —how good it is!
net.PRO_15_24: The path of life is upward for the wise person, to keep him from going downward to Sheol.
net.PRO_15_25: The Lord tears down the house of the proud, but he maintains the boundaries of the widow.
net.PRO_15_26: The Lord abhors the plans of the wicked, but pleasant words are pure.
net.PRO_15_27: The one who is greedy for gain troubles his household, but whoever hates bribes will live.
net.PRO_15_28: The heart of the righteous considers how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked pours out evil things.
net.PRO_15_29: The Lord is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
net.PRO_15_30: A bright look brings joy to the heart, and good news gives health to the body.
net.PRO_15_31: The person who hears the reproof that leads to life is at home among the wise.
net.PRO_15_32: The one who refuses correction despises himself, but whoever listens to reproof acquires understanding.
net.PRO_15_33: The fear of the Lord provides wise instruction, and before honor comes humility.
net.PRO_16_1: The intentions of the heart belong to a man, but the answer of the tongue comes from the Lord.
net.PRO_16_2: All a person’s ways seem right in his own opinion, but the Lord evaluates the motives.
net.PRO_16_3: Commit your works to the Lord, and your plans will be established.
net.PRO_16_4: The Lord has worked everything for his own ends — even the wicked for the day of disaster.
net.PRO_16_5: The Lord abhors every arrogant person; rest assured that they will not go unpunished.
net.PRO_16_6: Through loyal love and truth iniquity is appeased; through fearing the Lord one avoids evil.
net.PRO_16_7: When a person’s ways are pleasing to the Lord, he even reconciles his enemies to himself.
net.PRO_16_8: Better to have a little with righteousness than to have abundant income without justice.
net.PRO_16_9: A person plans his course, but the Lord directs his steps.
net.PRO_16_10: The divine verdict is in the words of the king, his pronouncements must not act treacherously against justice.
net.PRO_16_11: Honest scales and balances are from the Lord; all the weights in the bag are his handiwork.
net.PRO_16_12: Doing wickedness is an abomination to kings, because a throne is established in righteousness.
net.PRO_16_13: The delight of a king is righteous counsel, and he will love the one who speaks uprightly.
net.PRO_16_14: A king’s wrath is like a messenger of death, but a wise person appeases it.
net.PRO_16_15: In the light of the king’s face there is life, and his favor is like the clouds of the spring rain.
net.PRO_16_16: How much better it is to acquire wisdom than gold; to acquire understanding is more desirable than silver.
net.PRO_16_17: The highway of the upright is to turn away from evil; the one who guards his way safeguards his life.
net.PRO_16_18: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
net.PRO_16_19: It is better to be lowly in spirit with the afflicted than to share the spoils with the proud.
net.PRO_16_20: The one who deals wisely in a matter will find success, and blessed is the one who trusts in the Lord.
net.PRO_16_21: The one who is wise in heart is called discerning, and kind speech increases persuasiveness.
net.PRO_16_22: Insight is like a life-giving fountain to the one who possesses it, but folly leads to the discipline of fools.
net.PRO_16_23: A wise person’s heart makes his speech wise and it adds persuasiveness to his words.
net.PRO_16_24: Pleasant words are like a honeycomb, sweet to the soul and healing to the bones.
net.PRO_16_25: There is a way that seems right to a person, but its end is the way that leads to death.
net.PRO_16_26: A laborer’s appetite has labored for him, for his hunger has pressed him to work.
net.PRO_16_27: A wicked scoundrel digs up evil, and his slander is like a scorching fire.
net.PRO_16_28: A perverse person spreads dissension, and a gossip separates the closest friends.
net.PRO_16_29: A violent person entices his neighbor, and then leads him down a path that is terrible.
net.PRO_16_30: The one who winks his eyes devises perverse things, and one who compresses his lips has accomplished evil.
net.PRO_16_31: Gray hair is like a crown of glory; it is attained in the path of righteousness.
net.PRO_16_32: Better to be slow to anger than to be a mighty warrior, and one who controls his temper is better than one who captures a city.
net.PRO_16_33: The dice are thrown into the lap, but their every decision is from the Lord.
net.PRO_17_1: Better is a dry crust of bread where there is quietness than a house full of feasting with strife.
net.PRO_17_2: A servant who acts wisely will rule over an heir who behaves shamefully, and will share the inheritance along with the relatives.
net.PRO_17_3: The crucible is for refining silver and the furnace is for gold, likewise the Lord tests hearts.
net.PRO_17_4: One who acts wickedly pays attention to evil counsel; a liar listens to a malicious tongue.
net.PRO_17_5: The one who mocks the poor has insulted his Creator; whoever rejoices over disaster will not go unpunished.
net.PRO_17_6: Grandchildren are like a crown to the elderly, and the glory of children is their parents.
net.PRO_17_7: Excessive speech is not becoming for a fool; how much less are lies for a ruler!
net.PRO_17_8: A bribe works like a charm for the one who offers it; in whatever he does he succeeds.
net.PRO_17_9: The one who forgives an offense seeks love, but whoever repeats a matter separates close friends.
net.PRO_17_10: A rebuke makes a greater impression on a discerning person than a hundred blows on a fool.
net.PRO_17_11: An evil person seeks only rebellion, and so a cruel messenger will be sent against him.
net.PRO_17_12: It is better for a person to meet a mother bear being robbed of her cubs, than to encounter a fool in his folly.
net.PRO_17_13: As for the one who repays evil for good, evil will not leave his house.
net.PRO_17_14: Starting a quarrel is like letting out water; abandon strife before it breaks out!
net.PRO_17_15: The one who acquits the guilty and the one who condemns the innocent — both of them are an abomination to the Lord.
net.PRO_17_16: What’s the point of a fool having money in hand to buy wisdom, when his head is empty?
net.PRO_17_17: A friend loves at all times, and a relative is born to help in adversity.
net.PRO_17_18: The one who lacks sense strikes hands in pledge, and puts up financial security for his neighbor.
net.PRO_17_19: The one who loves a quarrel loves transgression; whoever builds his gate high seeks destruction.
net.PRO_17_20: The one who has a perverse heart does not find good, and the one who is deceitful in speech falls into trouble.
net.PRO_17_21: Whoever brings a fool into the world does so to his grief, and the father of a fool has no joy.
net.PRO_17_22: A cheerful heart brings good healing, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
net.PRO_17_23: A wicked person receives a bribe secretly to pervert the ways of justice.
net.PRO_17_24: Wisdom is directly in front of the discerning person, but the eyes of a fool run to the ends of the earth.
net.PRO_17_25: A foolish child is a grief to his father, and bitterness to the mother who bore him.
net.PRO_17_26: It is terrible to punish a righteous person, and to flog honorable men is wrong.
net.PRO_17_27: The truly wise person restrains his words, and the one who stays calm is discerning.
net.PRO_17_28: Even a fool who remains silent is considered wise, and the one who holds his tongue is deemed discerning.
net.PRO_18_1: One who has isolated himself seeks his own desires; he rejects all sound judgment.
net.PRO_18_2: A fool takes no pleasure in understanding but only in disclosing what is on his mind.
net.PRO_18_3: When a wicked person arrives, contempt shows up with him, and with shame comes a reproach.
net.PRO_18_4: The words of a person’s mouth are like deep waters, and the fountain of wisdom is like a flowing brook.
net.PRO_18_5: It is terrible to show partiality to the wicked, by depriving a righteous man of justice.
net.PRO_18_6: The lips of a fool enter into strife, and his mouth invites a flogging.
net.PRO_18_7: The mouth of a fool is his ruin, and his lips are a snare for his life.
net.PRO_18_8: The words of a gossip are like choice morsels; and they have gone down into the person’s innermost being.
net.PRO_18_9: The one who is slack in his work is a brother to one who destroys.
net.PRO_18_10: The name of the Lord is like a strong tower; the righteous person runs to it and is set safely on high.
net.PRO_18_11: The wealth of a rich person is like a strong city, and it is like a high wall in his imagination.
net.PRO_18_12: Before destruction the heart of a person is proud, but humility comes before honor.
net.PRO_18_13: The one who gives an answer before he listens — that is his folly and his shame.
net.PRO_18_14: A person’s spirit sustains him through sickness— but who can bear a crushed spirit?
net.PRO_18_15: The discerning person acquires knowledge, and the wise person seeks knowledge.
net.PRO_18_16: A person’s gift makes room for him, and leads him before important people.
net.PRO_18_17: The first to state his case seems right, until his opponent begins to cross-examine him.
net.PRO_18_18: A toss of a coin ends disputes, and settles the issue between strong opponents.
net.PRO_18_19: A relative offended is harder to reach than a strong city, and disputes are like the barred gates of a fortified citadel.
net.PRO_18_20: From the fruit of a person’s mouth his stomach will be satisfied, with the product of his lips he will be satisfied.
net.PRO_18_21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, and those who love its use will eat its fruit.
net.PRO_18_22: The one who has found a good wife has found what goodness is, and obtained a delightful gift from the Lord.
net.PRO_18_23: A poor person makes supplications, but a rich man answers harshly.
net.PRO_18_24: There are companions who harm one another, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.
net.PRO_19_1: Better is a poor person who walks in his integrity than one who is perverse in his speech and is a fool.
net.PRO_19_2: It is dangerous to have zeal without knowledge, and the one who acts hastily makes poor choices.
net.PRO_19_3: A person’s folly subverts his way, and his heart rages against the Lord.
net.PRO_19_4: Wealth adds many friends, but a poor person is separated from his friend.
net.PRO_19_5: A false witness will not go unpunished, and the one who spouts out lies will not escape punishment.
net.PRO_19_6: Many people entreat the favor of a generous person, and everyone is the friend of the person who gives gifts.
net.PRO_19_7: All the relatives of a poor person hate him; how much more do his friends avoid him— one who chases words, which are nothing.
net.PRO_19_8: The one who acquires understanding loves himself; the one who preserves understanding will prosper.
net.PRO_19_9: A false witness will not go unpunished, and the one who spouts out lies will perish.
net.PRO_19_10: Luxury is not appropriate for a fool; how much less for a servant to rule over princes!
net.PRO_19_11: A person’s wisdom has made him slow to anger, and it is his glory to overlook an offense.
net.PRO_19_12: A king’s wrath is like the roar of a lion, but his favor is like dew on the grass.
net.PRO_19_13: A foolish child is the ruin of his father, and a contentious wife is like a constant dripping.
net.PRO_19_14: A house and wealth are inherited from parents, but a prudent wife is from the Lord.
net.PRO_19_15: Laziness brings on a deep sleep, and the idle person will go hungry.
net.PRO_19_16: The one who obeys commandments guards his life; the one who despises his ways will die.
net.PRO_19_17: The one who is gracious to the poor lends to the Lord, and the Lord will repay him for his good deed.
net.PRO_19_18: Discipline your child, for there is hope, but do not set your heart on causing his death.
net.PRO_19_19: A person with great anger bears the penalty, but if you deliver him from it once, you will have to do it again.
net.PRO_19_20: Listen to advice and receive discipline, that you may become wise by the end of your life.
net.PRO_19_21: There are many plans in a person’s mind, but it is the counsel of the Lord that will stand.
net.PRO_19_22: What is desirable for a person is to show loyal love, and a poor person is better than a liar.
net.PRO_19_23: Fearing the Lord leads to life, and one who does so will live satisfied; he will not be afflicted by calamity.
net.PRO_19_24: The sluggard has plunged his hand into the dish, and he will not even bring it back to his mouth!
net.PRO_19_25: Flog a scorner, and as a result the simpleton will learn prudence; correct a discerning person, and as a result he will understand knowledge.
net.PRO_19_26: The one who robs his father and chases away his mother is a son who brings shame and disgrace.
net.PRO_19_27: If you stop listening to instruction, my child, you will stray from the words of knowledge.
net.PRO_19_28: A crooked witness scorns justice, and the mouth of the wicked devours iniquity.
net.PRO_19_29: Penalties have been prepared for scorners, and floggings for the backs of fools.
net.PRO_20_1: Wine is a mocker and strong drink is a brawler; whoever goes astray by them is not wise.
net.PRO_20_2: The king’s terrifying anger is like the roar of a lion; whoever provokes him sins against himself.
net.PRO_20_3: It is an honor for a person to cease from strife, but every fool quarrels.
net.PRO_20_4: The sluggard will not plow during the planting season, so at harvest time he asks for grain but has nothing.
net.PRO_20_5: Counsel in a person’s heart is like deep water, but an understanding person draws it out.
net.PRO_20_6: Many people profess their loyalty, but a faithful person —who can find?
net.PRO_20_7: The righteous person behaves in integrity; blessed are his children after him.
net.PRO_20_8: A king sitting on the throne to judge separates out all evil with his eyes.
net.PRO_20_9: Who can say, “I have kept my heart clean; I am pure from my sin”?
net.PRO_20_10: Diverse weights and diverse measures — the Lord abhors both of them.
net.PRO_20_11: Even a young man is known by his actions, whether his activity is pure and whether it is right.
net.PRO_20_12: The ear that hears and the eye that sees — the Lord has made them both.
net.PRO_20_13: Do not love sleep, lest you become impoverished; open your eyes so that you might be satisfied with food.
net.PRO_20_14: “It’s worthless! It’s worthless!” says the buyer, but when he goes on his way, he boasts.
net.PRO_20_15: There is gold, and an abundance of rubies, but words of knowledge are like a precious jewel.
net.PRO_20_16: Take a man’s garment when he has given security for a stranger, and hold him in pledge on behalf of strangers.
net.PRO_20_17: Bread gained by deceit tastes sweet to a person, but afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel.
net.PRO_20_18: Plans are established by counsel, so make war with guidance.
net.PRO_20_19: The one who goes about gossiping reveals secrets; therefore do not associate with someone who is always opening his mouth.
net.PRO_20_20: The one who curses his father and his mother, his lamp will be extinguished in the blackest darkness.
net.PRO_20_21: An inheritance gained easily in the beginning will not be blessed in the end.
net.PRO_20_22: Do not say, “I will pay back evil!” Wait for the Lord, so that he may vindicate you.
net.PRO_20_23: The Lord abhors differing weights, and dishonest scales are wicked.
net.PRO_20_24: The steps of a person are ordained by the Lord— so how can anyone understand his own way?
net.PRO_20_25: It is a snare for a person to rashly cry, “Holy!” and only afterward to consider what he has vowed.
net.PRO_20_26: A wise king separates out the wicked; he turns the threshing wheel over them.
net.PRO_20_27: The human spirit is like the lamp of the Lord, searching all his innermost parts.
net.PRO_20_28: Loyal love and truth preserve a king, and his throne is upheld by loyal love.
net.PRO_20_29: The glory of young men is their strength, and the splendor of old men is gray hair.
net.PRO_20_30: Beatings and wounds cleanse away evil, and floggings cleanse the innermost being.
net.PRO_21_1: The king’s heart is in the hand of the Lord like channels of water; he turns it wherever he wants.
net.PRO_21_2: All a person’s ways seem right in his own opinion, but the Lord evaluates his thoughts.
net.PRO_21_3: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to the Lord than sacrifice.
net.PRO_21_4: Haughty eyes and a proud heart— what the wicked cultivate is sin.
net.PRO_21_5: The plans of the diligent lead only to plenty, but everyone who is hasty comes only to poverty.
