net.PRO_21_6: Making a fortune by a lying tongue is like a vapor driven back and forth; they seek death.
net.PRO_21_7: The violence done by the wicked will drag them away because they have refused to do what is right.
net.PRO_21_8: The way of the guilty person is devious, but as for the pure, his way is upright.
net.PRO_21_9: It is better to live on a corner of the housetop than to share a house with a quarrelsome wife.
net.PRO_21_10: The appetite of the wicked has desired evil; his neighbor is shown no favor in his eyes.
net.PRO_21_11: When a scorner is punished, the naive becomes wise; when a wise person is instructed, he gains knowledge.
net.PRO_21_12: The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked; he overthrows the wicked to their ruin.
net.PRO_21_13: The one who shuts his ears to the cry of the poor, he too will cry out and will not be answered.
net.PRO_21_14: A gift given in secret subdues anger, and a bribe given secretly subdues strong wrath.
net.PRO_21_15: Doing justice brings joy to the righteous and terror to those who do evil.
net.PRO_21_16: The one who wanders from the way of wisdom will end up in the company of the departed.
net.PRO_21_17: The one who loves pleasure will be a poor person; whoever loves wine and anointing oil will not be rich.
net.PRO_21_18: The wicked become a ransom for the righteous, and the treacherous are taken in the place of the upright.
net.PRO_21_19: It is better to live in the wilderness than with a quarrelsome and easily provoked woman.
net.PRO_21_20: There is desirable treasure and olive oil in the dwelling of the wise, but a foolish person devours all he has.
net.PRO_21_21: The one who pursues righteousness and love finds life, bounty, and honor.
net.PRO_21_22: A wise man went up against the city of the mighty and brought down the stronghold in which they trust.
net.PRO_21_23: The one who guards his mouth and his tongue keeps his life from troubles.
net.PRO_21_24: A proud and arrogant person, whose name is “Scoffer,” acts with overbearing pride.
net.PRO_21_25: What the sluggard desires will kill him, for his hands have refused to work.
net.PRO_21_26: All day long he has craved greedily, but the righteous person gives and does not hold back.
net.PRO_21_27: The wicked person’s sacrifice is an abomination; how much more when he brings it with evil intent!
net.PRO_21_28: A lying witness will perish, but the one who reports accurately speaks forever.
net.PRO_21_29: A wicked person has put on a bold face, but as for the upright, he establishes his ways.
net.PRO_21_30: There is no wisdom and there is no understanding, and there is no counsel against the Lord.
net.PRO_21_31: A horse is prepared for the day of battle, but the victory is from the Lord.
net.PRO_22_1: A good name is to be chosen rather than great wealth, good favor more than silver or gold.
net.PRO_22_2: The rich and the poor are met together; the Lord is the Creator of them both.
net.PRO_22_3: A shrewd person saw danger and hid himself, but the naive passed on by and paid for it.
net.PRO_22_4: The reward for humility and fearing the Lord is riches and honor and life.
net.PRO_22_5: Thorns and snares are in the path of the perverse, but the one who guards himself keeps far from them.
net.PRO_22_6: Train a child in the way that he should go, and when he is old he will not turn from it.
net.PRO_22_7: The rich rule over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender.
net.PRO_22_8: The one who sows iniquity will reap trouble, and the rod of his fury will end.
net.PRO_22_9: A generous person will be blessed, for he has given some of his food to the poor.
net.PRO_22_10: Drive out the scorner and contention will leave; strife and insults will cease.
net.PRO_22_11: The one who loves a pure heart and whose speech is gracious —the king will be his friend.
net.PRO_22_12: The eyes of the Lord watched over a cause, and subverted the words of the treacherous person.
net.PRO_22_13: The sluggard has said, “There is a lion outside! I will be killed in the middle of the streets!”
net.PRO_22_14: The mouth of an adulteress is like a deep pit; the one against whom the Lord is angry will fall into it.
net.PRO_22_15: Folly is bound up in the heart of a child, but the rod of discipline will drive it far from him.
net.PRO_22_16: The one who oppresses the poor to increase his own gain and the one who gives to the rich —both end up only in poverty. The Sayings of the Wise
net.PRO_22_17: Incline your ear and listen to the words of the wise, and apply your mind to my instruction.
net.PRO_22_18: For it is pleasing if you keep these sayings within you, and they are ready on your lips.
net.PRO_22_19: So that your confidence may be in the Lord, I hereby make them known to you today—even you.
net.PRO_22_20: Have I not written thirty sayings for you, sayings of counsel and knowledge,
net.PRO_22_21: to show you true and reliable words, so that you may give accurate answers to those who sent you?
net.PRO_22_22: Do not exploit a poor person because he is poor and do not crush the needy in court,
net.PRO_22_23: for the Lord will plead their case and will rob the life of those who are robbing them.
net.PRO_22_24: Do not make friends with an angry person, and do not associate with a wrathful person,
net.PRO_22_25: lest you learn his ways and entangle yourself in a snare.
net.PRO_22_26: Do not be one who strikes hands in pledge or who puts up security for debts.
net.PRO_22_27: If you do not have enough to pay, your bed will be taken right out from under you!
net.PRO_22_28: Do not move an ancient boundary stone that was put in place by your ancestors.
net.PRO_22_29: You have seen a person skilled in his work— he will take his position before kings; he will not take his position before obscure people.
net.PRO_23_1: When you sit down to eat with a ruler, consider carefully what is before you,
net.PRO_23_2: and put a knife to your throat if you possess a large appetite.
net.PRO_23_3: Do not crave that ruler’s delicacies, for that food is deceptive.
net.PRO_23_4: Do not wear yourself out to become rich; be wise enough to restrain yourself.
net.PRO_23_5: When you gaze upon riches, they are gone, for they surely make wings for themselves, and fly off into the sky like an eagle!
net.PRO_23_6: Do not eat the food of a stingy person, do not crave his delicacies;
net.PRO_23_7: for he is like someone who has calculated the cost in his mind. “Eat and drink,” he says to you, but his heart is not with you;
net.PRO_23_8: you will vomit up the little bit you have eaten, and will have wasted your pleasant words.
net.PRO_23_9: Do not speak in the ears of a fool, for he will despise the wisdom of your words.
net.PRO_23_10: Do not move an ancient boundary stone, or take over the fields of the fatherless,
net.PRO_23_11: for their Protector is strong; he will plead their case against you.
net.PRO_23_12: Apply your heart to instruction and your ears to the words of knowledge.
net.PRO_23_13: Do not withhold discipline from a child; even if you strike him with the rod, he will not die.
net.PRO_23_14: If you strike him with the rod, you will deliver him from death.
net.PRO_23_15: My child, if your heart is wise, then my heart also will be glad;
net.PRO_23_16: my soul will rejoice when your lips speak what is right.
net.PRO_23_17: Do not let your heart envy sinners, but rather be zealous in fearing the Lord all the time.
net.PRO_23_18: For surely there is a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
net.PRO_23_19: Listen, my child, and be wise, and guide your heart on the right way.
net.PRO_23_20: Do not spend time among drunkards, among those who eat too much meat,
net.PRO_23_21: because drunkards and gluttons become impoverished, and drowsiness clothes them with rags.
net.PRO_23_22: Listen to your father who gave you life, and do not despise your mother when she is old.
net.PRO_23_23: Acquire truth and do not sell it— wisdom, and discipline, and understanding.
net.PRO_23_24: The father of a righteous person will rejoice greatly; whoever fathers a wise child will have joy in him.
net.PRO_23_25: May your father and your mother have joy; may she who bore you rejoice.
net.PRO_23_26: Give me your heart, my son, and let your eyes observe my ways;
net.PRO_23_27: for a prostitute is like a deep pit; a harlot is like a narrow well.
net.PRO_23_28: Indeed, she lies in wait like a robber, and increases the unfaithful among men.
net.PRO_23_29: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has contentions? Who has complaints? Who has wounds without cause? Who has dullness of the eyes?
net.PRO_23_30: Those who linger over wine, those who go looking for mixed wine.
net.PRO_23_31: Do not look on the wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup, when it goes down smoothly.
net.PRO_23_32: Afterward it bites like a snake, and stings like a viper.
net.PRO_23_33: Your eyes will see strange things, and your mind will speak perverse things.
net.PRO_23_34: And you will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea, and like one who lies down on the top of the rigging.
net.PRO_23_35: You will say, “They have struck me, but I am not harmed! They beat me, but I did not know it! When will I awake? I will look for another drink.”
net.PRO_24_1: Do not envy evil people, do not desire to be with them;
net.PRO_24_2: for their hearts contemplate violence, and their lips speak harm.
net.PRO_24_3: By wisdom a house is built, and through understanding it is established;
net.PRO_24_4: by knowledge its rooms are filled with all kinds of precious and pleasing treasures.
net.PRO_24_5: A wise warrior is strong, and a man of knowledge makes his strength stronger;
net.PRO_24_6: for with guidance you wage your war, and with numerous advisers there is victory.
net.PRO_24_7: Wisdom is unattainable for a fool; in court he does not open his mouth.
net.PRO_24_8: The one who plans to do evil will be called a scheming person.
net.PRO_24_9: A foolish scheme is sin, and the scorner is an abomination to people.
net.PRO_24_10: You have slacked off in the day of trouble — your strength is small!
net.PRO_24_11: Deliver those being taken away to death, and hold back those slipping to the slaughter.
net.PRO_24_12: If you say, “But we did not know about this,” won’t the one who evaluates hearts discern it? Won’t the one who guards your life realize and repay each person according to his deeds?
net.PRO_24_13: Eat honey, my child, for it is good, and honey from the honeycomb is sweet to your taste.
net.PRO_24_14: Likewise, know that wisdom is sweet to your soul; if you have found it, you have a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
net.PRO_24_15: Do not lie in wait like the wicked against the place where the righteous live; do not assault his home.
net.PRO_24_16: Indeed a righteous person will fall seven times, and then get up again, but the guilty will collapse in calamity.
net.PRO_24_17: Do not rejoice when your enemy falls, and when he stumbles do not let your heart rejoice,
net.PRO_24_18: lest the Lord see it, and be displeased, and turn his wrath away from him.
net.PRO_24_19: Do not fret because of evil people or be envious of wicked people,
net.PRO_24_20: for the evil person has no future, and the lamp of the wicked will be extinguished.
net.PRO_24_21: Fear the Lord, my child, as well as the king, and do not associate with rebels,
net.PRO_24_22: for suddenly their destruction will overtake them, and who knows the ruinous judgment both the Lord and the king can bring? Further Sayings of the Wise
net.PRO_24_23: These sayings also are from the wise: To show partiality in judgment is terrible:
net.PRO_24_24: The one who says to the guilty, “You are innocent,” peoples will curse him, and nations will denounce him.
net.PRO_24_25: But there will be delight for those who convict the guilty, and a pleasing blessing will come on them.
net.PRO_24_26: Like a kiss on the lips is the one who gives an honest answer.
net.PRO_24_27: Establish your work outside and get your fields ready; afterward build your house.
net.PRO_24_28: Do not be a witness against your neighbor without cause, and do not deceive with your words.
net.PRO_24_29: Do not say, “I will do to him just as he has done to me; I will pay him back according to what he has done.”
net.PRO_24_30: I passed by the field of a sluggard, by the vineyard of one who lacks sense.
net.PRO_24_31: I saw that thorns had grown up all over it, the ground was covered with weeds, and its stone wall was broken down.
net.PRO_24_32: Then I scrutinized it. I was putting my mind to it— I saw; I took in a lesson:
net.PRO_24_33: “A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to relax,
net.PRO_24_34: and your poverty will come like a bandit, and your need like an armed robber.”
net.PRO_25_1: These also are proverbs of Solomon, which the men of King Hezekiah of Judah copied:
net.PRO_25_2: It is the glory of God to conceal a matter, and it is the glory of a king to search out a matter.
net.PRO_25_3: As the heaven is high and the earth is deep so the hearts of kings are unsearchable.
net.PRO_25_4: Remove the dross from the silver, and material for the silversmith will emerge;
net.PRO_25_5: remove the wicked from before the king, and his throne will be established in righteousness.
net.PRO_25_6: Do not honor yourself before the king, and do not stand in the place of great men;
net.PRO_25_7: for it is better for him to say to you, “Come up here,” than to put you lower before a prince, whom your eyes have seen.
net.PRO_25_8: Do not go out hastily to litigation, or what will you do afterward when your neighbor puts you to shame?
net.PRO_25_9: When you argue a case with your neighbor, do not reveal the secret of another person,
net.PRO_25_10: lest the one who hears it put you to shame and your infamy will never go away.
net.PRO_25_11: Like apples of gold in settings of silver, so is a word skillfully spoken.
net.PRO_25_12: Like an earring of gold and an ornament of fine gold, so is a wise reprover to the ear of the one who listens.
net.PRO_25_13: Like the cold of snow in the time of harvest, so is a faithful messenger to those who send him, for he refreshes the heart of his masters.
net.PRO_25_14: Like cloudy skies and wind that produce no rain, so is the one who boasts of a gift not given.
net.PRO_25_15: Through patience a ruler can be persuaded, and a soft tongue can break a bone.
net.PRO_25_16: You have found honey—eat only what is sufficient for you, lest you become stuffed with it and vomit it up.
net.PRO_25_17: Don’t set foot too frequently in your neighbor’s house, lest he become weary of you and hate you.
net.PRO_25_18: Like a club or a sword or a sharp arrow, so is the one who testifies against his neighbor as a false witness.
net.PRO_25_19: Like a bad tooth or a foot out of joint, so is confidence in an unfaithful person at the time of trouble.
net.PRO_25_20: Like one who takes off a garment on a cold day, or like vinegar poured on soda, so is one who sings songs to a heavy heart.
net.PRO_25_21: If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat, and if he is thirsty, give him water to drink,
net.PRO_25_22: for you will heap coals of fire on his head, and the Lord will reward you.
net.PRO_25_23: The north wind brings forth rain, and a gossiping tongue brings forth an angry look.
net.PRO_25_24: It is better to live on a corner of the housetop than in a house in company with a quarrelsome wife.
net.PRO_25_25: Like cold water to a weary person, so is good news from a distant land.
net.PRO_25_26: Like a muddied spring and a polluted well, so is a righteous person who gives way before the wicked.
net.PRO_25_27: It is not good to eat too much honey, nor is it honorable for people to seek their own glory.
net.PRO_25_28: Like a city that is broken down and without a wall, so is a person who cannot control his temper.
net.PRO_26_1: Like snow in summer or rain in harvest, so honor is not fitting for a fool.
net.PRO_26_2: Like a fluttering bird or like a flying swallow, so a curse without cause does not come to rest.
net.PRO_26_3: A whip for the horse and a bridle for the donkey, and a rod for the backs of fools!
net.PRO_26_4: Do not answer a fool according to his folly, lest you yourself also be like him.
net.PRO_26_5: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own opinion.
net.PRO_26_6: Like cutting off the feet or drinking violence, so is sending a message by the hand of a fool.
net.PRO_26_7: Like legs dangle uselessly from the lame, so a proverb dangles in the mouth of fools.
net.PRO_26_8: Like tying a stone in a sling, so is giving honor to a fool.
net.PRO_26_9: Like a thorn has gone up into the hand of a drunkard, so a proverb has gone up into the mouth of a fool.
net.PRO_26_10: Like an archer who wounds at random, so is the one who hires a fool or hires any passerby.
net.PRO_26_11: Like a dog that returns to its vomit, so a fool repeats his folly.
net.PRO_26_12: You have seen a man wise in his own opinion — there is more hope for a fool than for him.
net.PRO_26_13: The sluggard has said, “There is a lion in the road! A lion in the streets!”
net.PRO_26_14: Like a door that turns on its hinges, so a sluggard turns on his bed.
net.PRO_26_15: The sluggard has plunged his hand in the dish; he is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth.
net.PRO_26_16: The sluggard is wiser in his own opinion than seven people who respond with good sense.
net.PRO_26_17: Like one who grabs a wild dog by the ears, so is the person passing by who becomes furious over a quarrel not his own.
net.PRO_26_18: Like a madman who shoots firebrands and deadly arrows,
net.PRO_26_19: so is a person who has deceived his neighbor, and said, “Was I not only joking?”
net.PRO_26_20: Where there is no wood, a fire goes out, and where there is no gossip, contention ceases.
net.PRO_26_21: Like charcoal is to burning coals, and wood to fire, so is a contentious person to kindle strife.
net.PRO_26_22: The words of a gossip are like choice morsels; and they have gone down into a person’s innermost being.
net.PRO_26_23: Like a coating of glaze over earthenware are fervent lips with an evil heart.
net.PRO_26_24: The one who hates others disguises it with his lips, but he stores up deceit within him.
net.PRO_26_25: When he speaks graciously, do not believe him, for there are seven abominations within him.
net.PRO_26_26: Though his hatred may be concealed by deceit, his evil will be uncovered in the assembly.
net.PRO_26_27: The one who digs a pit will fall into it; the one who rolls a stone—it will come back on him.
net.PRO_26_28: A lying tongue hates those crushed by it, and a flattering mouth works ruin.
net.PRO_27_1: Do not boast about tomorrow; for you do not know what a day may bring forth.
net.PRO_27_2: Let another praise you, and not your own mouth; someone else, and not your own lips.
net.PRO_27_3: A stone is heavy and sand is weighty, but vexation by a fool is more burdensome than the two of them.
net.PRO_27_4: Wrath is cruel and anger is overwhelming, but who can stand before jealousy?
net.PRO_27_5: Better is open rebuke than hidden love.
net.PRO_27_6: Faithful are the wounds of a friend, but the kisses of an enemy are excessive.
net.PRO_27_7: The one whose appetite is satisfied loathes honey, but to the hungry mouth every bitter thing is sweet.
net.PRO_27_8: Like a bird that wanders from its nest, so is a person who wanders from his home.
net.PRO_27_9: Ointment and incense make the heart rejoice, likewise the sweetness of one’s friend from sincere counsel.
net.PRO_27_10: Do not forsake your friend and your father’s friend, and do not enter your brother’s house in the day of your disaster; a neighbor nearby is better than a brother far away.
net.PRO_27_11: Be wise, my son, and make my heart glad, so that I may answer anyone who taunts me.
net.PRO_27_12: A shrewd person saw danger—he hid himself; the naive passed right on by — they had to pay for it.
net.PRO_27_13: Take a man’s garment when he has given security for a stranger, and hold him in pledge on behalf of a stranger.
net.PRO_27_14: If someone blesses his neighbor with a loud voice early in the morning, it will be counted as a curse to him.
net.PRO_27_15: A continual dripping on a rainy day— a contentious wife makes herself like that.
net.PRO_27_16: Whoever contains her has contained the wind or can grasp oil with his right hand.
net.PRO_27_17: As iron sharpens iron, so a person sharpens his friend.
net.PRO_27_18: The one who tends a fig tree will eat its fruit, and whoever takes care of his master will be honored.
net.PRO_27_19: As in water the face is reflected as a face, so a person’s heart reflects the person.
net.PRO_27_20: As Death and Destruction are never satisfied, so the eyes of a person are never satisfied.
net.PRO_27_21: As the crucible is for silver and the furnace is for gold, so a person must put his praise to the test.
net.PRO_27_22: If you should pound the fool in the mortar among the grain with the pestle, his foolishness would not depart from him.
net.PRO_27_23: Pay careful attention to the condition of your flocks, set your mind on your herds,
net.PRO_27_24: for riches do not last forever, nor does a crown last from generation to generation.
net.PRO_27_25: When the hay is removed and new grass appears, and the grass from the hills is gathered in,
net.PRO_27_26: the lambs will be for your clothing, and the goats will be for the price of a field.
net.PRO_27_27: And there will be enough goat’s milk for your food, for the food of your household, and for the sustenance of your servant girls.
net.PRO_28_1: The wicked person fled, though no one was pursuing, but the righteous person can be as confident as a lion.
net.PRO_28_2: When a country is rebellious it has many princes, but by someone who is discerning and knowledgeable order is maintained.
net.PRO_28_3: A poor person who oppresses the weak is like a driving rain without food.
net.PRO_28_4: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked, but those who keep the law contend with them.
net.PRO_28_5: Evil people do not understand justice, but those who seek the Lord understand it all.
net.PRO_28_6: A poor person who walks in his integrity is better than one who is perverse in his ways even though he is rich.
net.PRO_28_7: The one who keeps the law is a discerning child, but a companion of gluttons brings shame to his parents.
net.PRO_28_8: The one who increases his wealth by increasing interest gathers it for someone who is gracious to the needy.
net.PRO_28_9: The one who turns away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.
net.PRO_28_10: The one who leads the upright astray in an evil way will himself fall into his own pit, but the blameless will inherit what is good.
net.PRO_28_11: A rich person is wise in his own opinion, but a discerning poor person can evaluate him properly.
net.PRO_28_12: When the righteous rejoice, great is the glory, but when the wicked rise to power, people are sought out.
net.PRO_28_13: The one who covers his transgressions will not prosper, but whoever confesses them and forsakes them will find mercy.
net.PRO_28_14: Blessed is the one who is always cautious, but whoever hardens his heart will fall into evil.
net.PRO_28_15: Like a roaring lion or a roving bear, so is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
net.PRO_28_16: The prince who is a great oppressor lacks wisdom, but the one who hates unjust gain will prolong his days.
net.PRO_28_17: The one who is tormented by the murder of another will flee to the pit; let no one support him.
net.PRO_28_18: The one who walks blamelessly will be delivered, but whoever is perverse in his ways will fall at once.
net.PRO_28_19: The one who works his land will be satisfied with food, but whoever chases daydreams will have his fill of poverty.
net.PRO_28_20: A faithful person will have an abundance of blessings, but the one who hastens to gain riches will not go unpunished.
net.PRO_28_21: To show partiality is terrible, for a person will transgress over the smallest piece of bread.
net.PRO_28_22: The stingy person hastens after riches and does not know that poverty will overtake him.
net.PRO_28_23: The one who reproves another will in the end find more favor than the one who flatters with the tongue.
net.PRO_28_24: The one who robs his father and mother and says, “There is no transgression,” is a companion to the one who destroys.
net.PRO_28_25: The greedy person stirs up dissension, but the one who trusts in the Lord will prosper.
net.PRO_28_26: The one who trusts in his own heart is a fool, but the one who walks in wisdom will escape.
net.PRO_28_27: The one who gives to the poor will not lack, but whoever shuts his eyes to them will receive many curses.
net.PRO_28_28: When the wicked gain control, people hide themselves, but when they perish, the righteous increase.
net.PRO_29_1: The one who stiffens his neck after numerous rebukes will suddenly be destroyed without remedy.
net.PRO_29_2: When the righteous become numerous, the people rejoice; when the wicked rule, the people groan.
net.PRO_29_3: The man who loves wisdom brings joy to his father, but whoever associates with prostitutes wastes his wealth.
net.PRO_29_4: A king brings stability to a land by justice, but one who exacts tribute tears it down.
net.PRO_29_5: The one who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his steps.
net.PRO_29_6: In the transgression of an evil person there is a snare, but a righteous person can sing and rejoice.
net.PRO_29_7: The righteous person cares for the legal rights of the poor; the wicked person does not understand such knowledge.
net.PRO_29_8: Scornful people inflame a city, but those who are wise turn away wrath.
net.PRO_29_9: When a wise person goes to court with a foolish person, there is no peace whether he is angry or laughs.
net.PRO_29_10: Bloodthirsty people hate someone with integrity; as for the upright, they seek his life.
net.PRO_29_11: A fool lets fly with all his temper, but a wise person keeps it back.
net.PRO_29_12: If a ruler listens to lies, all his ministers will be wicked.
net.PRO_29_13: The poor person and the oppressor have this in common: the Lord gives light to the eyes of them both.
net.PRO_29_14: If a king judges the poor in truth, his throne will be established forever.
net.PRO_29_15: A rod and reproof impart wisdom, but a child who is unrestrained brings shame to his mother.
net.PRO_29_16: When the wicked increase, transgression increases, but the righteous will see their downfall.
net.PRO_29_17: Discipline your child, and he will give you rest; he will bring you happiness.
net.PRO_29_18: When there is no prophetic vision the people cast off restraint, but the one who keeps the law, blessed is he!
net.PRO_29_19: A servant cannot be corrected by words, for although he understands, there is no answer.
net.PRO_29_20: You have seen someone who is hasty in his words — there is more hope for a fool than for him.
net.PRO_29_21: If someone pampers his servant from youth, he will be a weakling in the end.
net.PRO_29_22: An angry person stirs up dissension, and a wrathful person is abounding in transgression.
net.PRO_29_23: A person’s pride will bring him low, but one who has a lowly spirit will gain honor.
net.PRO_29_24: Whoever shares with a thief is his own enemy; he hears the oath to testify, but does not talk.
net.PRO_29_25: The fear of people becomes a snare, but whoever trusts in the Lord will be set on high.
net.PRO_29_26: Many people seek the face of a ruler, but it is from the Lord that one receives justice.
net.PRO_29_27: An unjust person is an abomination to the righteous, and the one who lives an upright life is an abomination to the wicked.
net.PRO_30_1: The words of Agur, the son of Jakeh; an oracle: This man says to Ithiel, to Ithiel and to Ukal:
net.PRO_30_2: Surely I am more brutish than any other human being, and I do not have human understanding;
net.PRO_30_3: I have not learned wisdom, nor can I have knowledge of the Holy One.
net.PRO_30_4: Who has ascended into heaven, and then descended? Who has gathered up the winds in his fists? Who has bound up the waters in his cloak? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is his son’s name? Surely you can know!
net.PRO_30_5: Every word of God is purified; he is like a shield for those who take refuge in him.
net.PRO_30_6: Do not add to his words, lest he reprove you, and prove you to be a liar.
net.PRO_30_7: Two things I have asked from you; do not refuse me before I die:
net.PRO_30_8: Remove falsehood and lies far from me; do not give me poverty or riches, feed me with my allotted portion of bread,
net.PRO_30_9: lest I become satisfied and act deceptively and say, “Who is the Lord?” Or lest I become poor and steal and demean the name of my God.
net.PRO_30_10: Do not slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you, and you are found guilty.
net.PRO_30_11: There is a generation who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers.
net.PRO_30_12: There is a generation who are pure in their own opinion and yet are not washed from their filthiness.
net.PRO_30_13: There is a generation whose eyes are so lofty, and whose eyelids are lifted up disdainfully.
net.PRO_30_14: There is a generation whose teeth are like swords and whose molars are like knives to devour the poor from the earth and the needy from among the human race.
net.PRO_30_15: The leech has two daughters: “Give! Give!” There are three things that will never be satisfied, four that have never said, “Enough” —
net.PRO_30_16: the grave, the barren womb; earth has not been satisfied with water; and fire has never said, “Enough!”
net.PRO_30_17: The eye that mocks at a father and despises obeying a mother— the ravens of the valley will peck it out and the young vultures will eat it.
net.PRO_30_18: There are three things that are too wonderful for me, four that I do not understand:
net.PRO_30_19: the way of an eagle in the sky, the way of a snake on a rock, the way of a ship in the sea, and the way of a man with a woman.
net.PRO_30_20: This is the way of an adulterous woman: she has eaten and wiped her mouth and has said, “I have not done wrong.”
net.PRO_30_21: Under three things the earth has trembled, and under four things it cannot bear up:
net.PRO_30_22: under a servant who becomes king, under a fool who becomes stuffed with food,
net.PRO_30_23: under an unloved woman who becomes married, and under a female servant who dispossesses her mistress.
net.PRO_30_24: There are four things on earth that are small, but they are exceedingly wise:
net.PRO_30_25: ants are creatures with little strength, but they prepare their food in the summer;
net.PRO_30_26: rock badgers are creatures with little power, but they make their homes in the crags;
net.PRO_30_27: locusts have no king, but they all go forward by ranks;
net.PRO_30_28: a lizard you can catch with the hand, but it gets into the palaces of the king.
net.PRO_30_29: There are three things that are magnificent in their step, four things that move about magnificently:
net.PRO_30_30: a lion, mightiest of the beasts, who does not retreat from anything;
net.PRO_30_31: a strutting rooster, a male goat, and a king with his army around him.
net.PRO_30_32: If you have done foolishly by exalting yourself or if you have planned evil, put your hand over your mouth!
net.PRO_30_33: For as the churning of milk produces butter and as punching the nose produces blood, so stirring up anger produces strife.
net.PRO_31_1: The words of King Lemuel, an oracle that his mother taught him:
net.PRO_31_2: O my son, O son of my womb, O son of my vows,
net.PRO_31_3: do not give your strength to women, nor your ways to that which ruins kings.
net.PRO_31_4: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, or for rulers to crave strong drink,
net.PRO_31_5: lest they drink and forget what is decreed, and remove from all the poor their legal rights.
net.PRO_31_6: Give strong drink to the one who is perishing, and wine to those who are bitterly distressed;
net.PRO_31_7: let them drink and forget their poverty, and remember their misery no more.
net.PRO_31_8: Open your mouth on behalf of those unable to speak, for the legal rights of all the dying.
net.PRO_31_9: Open your mouth, judge in righteousness, and plead the cause of the poor and needy. The Wife of Noble Character
net.PRO_31_10: Who can find a wife of noble character? For her value is far more than rubies.
net.PRO_31_11: Her husband’s heart has trusted her, and he does not lack the dividends.
net.PRO_31_12: She has rewarded him with good and not harm all the days of her life.
net.PRO_31_13: She sought out wool and flax, then worked happily with her hands.
net.PRO_31_14: She was like the merchant ships; she would bring in her food from afar.
net.PRO_31_15: Then she rose while it was still night, and provided food for her household and a portion to her female servants.
net.PRO_31_16: She considered a field and bought it; from her own income she planted a vineyard.
net.PRO_31_17: She clothed herself in might, and she strengthened her arms.
net.PRO_31_18: She perceived that her merchandise was good. Her lamp would not go out in the night.
net.PRO_31_19: She extended her hands to the spool, and her hands grasped the spindle.
net.PRO_31_20: She opened her hand to the poor, and extended her hands to the needy.
net.PRO_31_21: She would not fear for her household in winter, because all her household were clothed with scarlet,
net.PRO_31_22: because she had made coverings for herself; and because her clothing was fine linen and purple.
net.PRO_31_23: Her husband is well-known in the city gate when he sits with the elders of the land.
net.PRO_31_24: She made linen garments then sold them, and traded belts to the merchants;
net.PRO_31_25: her clothing was strong and splendid; and she laughed at the time to come.
net.PRO_31_26: She has opened her mouth with wisdom, with loving instruction on her tongue.
net.PRO_31_27: Watching over the ways of her household, she would not eat the bread of idleness.
net.PRO_31_28: Her children have risen and called her blessed; her husband also has praised her:
net.PRO_31_29: “Many daughters have done valiantly, but you have surpassed them all!”
net.PRO_31_30: Charm is deceitful and beauty is fleeting. A woman who fears the Lord —she makes herself praiseworthy.
net.PRO_31_31: Give her credit for what she has accomplished, and let her works praise her in the city gates.
